NKJV.1: Then they came to the other side of the sea, to the country of the Gadarenes.
NKJV.2: And when He had come out of the boat, immediately there met Him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit,
NKJV.3: who had his dwelling among the tombs; and no one could bind him, not even with chains,
NKJV.4: because he had often been bound with shackles and chains. And the chains had been pulled apart by him, and the shackles broken in pieces; neither could anyone tame him.
NKJV.5: And always, night and day, he was in the mountains and in the tombs, crying out and cutting himself with stones.
NKJV.6: When he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and worshiped Him.
NKJV.7: And he cried out with a loud voice and said, “What have I to do with You, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I implore You by God that You do not torment me.”
NKJV.8: For He said to him, “Come out of the man, unclean spirit!”
NKJV.9: Then He asked him, “What is your name?” And he answered, saying, “My name is Legion; for we are many.”
NKJV.10: Also he begged Him earnestly that He would not send them out of the country.
NKJV.11: Now a large herd of swine was feeding there near the mountains.
NKJV.12: So all the demons begged Him, saying, “Send us to the swine, that we may enter them.”
NKJV.13: And at once Jesus gave them permission. Then the unclean spirits went out and entered the swine (there were about two thousand); and the herd ran violently down the steep place into the sea, and drowned in the sea.
NKJV.14: So those who fed the swine fled, and they told it in the city and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that had happened.
NKJV.15: Then they came to Jesus, and saw the one who had been demon-possessed and had the legion, sitting and clothed and in his right mind. And they were afraid.
NKJV.16: And those who saw it told them how it happened to him who had been demon-possessed, and about the swine.
NKJV.17: Then they began to plead with Him to depart from their region.
NKJV.18: And when He got into the boat, he who had been demon-possessed begged Him that he might be with Him.
NKJV.19: However, Jesus did not permit him, but said to him, “Go home to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how He has had compassion on you.”
NKJV.20: And he departed and began to proclaim in Decapolis all that Jesus had done for him; and all marveled.
NKJV.21: Now when Jesus had crossed over again by boat to the other side, a great multitude gathered to Him; and He was by the sea.
NKJV.22: And behold, one of the rulers of the synagogue came, Jairus by name. And when he saw Him, he fell at His feet
NKJV.23: and begged Him earnestly, saying, “My little daughter lies at the point of death. Come and lay Your hands on her, that she may be healed, and she will live.”
NKJV.24: So Jesus went with him, and a great multitude followed Him and thronged Him.
NKJV.25: Now a certain woman had a flow of blood for twelve years,
NKJV.26: and had suffered many things from many physicians. She had spent all that she had and was no better, but rather grew worse.
NKJV.27: When she heard about Jesus, she came behind Him in the crowd and touched His garment.
NKJV.28: For she said, “If only I may touch His clothes, I shall be made well.”
NKJV.29: Immediately the fountain of her blood was dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed of the affliction.
NKJV.30: And Jesus, immediately knowing in Himself that power had gone out of Him, turned around in the crowd and said, “Who touched My clothes?”
NKJV.31: But His disciples said to Him, “You see the multitude thronging You, and You say, ‘Who touched Me?’ ”
NKJV.32: And He looked around to see her who had done this thing.
NKJV.33: But the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell down before Him and told Him the whole truth.
NKJV.34: And He said to her, “Daughter,  your faith has made you well.  Go in peace, and be healed of your affliction.”
NKJV.35: While He was still speaking, some came from the ruler of the synagogue’s house who said, “Your daughter is dead. Why trouble the Teacher any further?”
NKJV.36: As soon as Jesus heard the word that was spoken, He said to the ruler of the synagogue, “Do not be afraid; only  believe.”
NKJV.37: And He permitted no one to follow Him except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.
NKJV.38: Then He came to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and saw a tumult and those who wept and wailed loudly.
NKJV.39: When He came in, He said to them, “Why make this commotion and weep? The child is not dead, but  sleeping.”
NKJV.40: And they ridiculed Him. But when He had put them all outside, He took the father and the mother of the child, and those who were with Him, and entered where the child was lying.
NKJV.41: Then He took the child by the hand, and said to her, “Talitha, cumi,” which is translated, “Little girl, I say to you, arise.”
NKJV.42: Immediately the girl arose and walked, for she was twelve years of age. And they were overcome with great amazement.
NKJV.43: But He commanded them strictly that no one should know it, and said that something should be given her to eat.

ESV.1: They came to the other side of the sea, to the country of the Gerasenes.
ESV.2: And when Jesus  had stepped out of the boat, immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit.
ESV.3: He lived among the tombs. And no one could bind him anymore, not even with a chain,
ESV.4: for he had often been bound with shackles and chains, but he wrenched the chains apart, and he broke the shackles in pieces. No one had the strength to subdue him.
ESV.5: Night and day among the tombs and on the mountains he was always crying out and cutting himself with stones.
ESV.6: And when he saw Jesus from afar, he ran and fell down before him.
ESV.7: And crying out with a loud voice, he said, “What have you to do with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I adjure you by God, do not torment me.”
ESV.8: For he was saying to him, “Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!”
ESV.9: And Jesus asked him, “What is your name?” He replied, “My name is Legion, for we are many.”
ESV.10: And he begged him earnestly not to send them out of the country.
ESV.11: Now a great herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside,
ESV.12: and they begged him, saying, “Send us to the pigs; let us enter them.”
ESV.13: So he gave them permission. And the unclean spirits came out and entered the pigs; and the herd, numbering about two thousand, rushed down the steep bank into the sea and drowned in the sea.
ESV.14: The herdsmen fled and told it in the city and in the country. And people came to see what it was that had happened.
ESV.15: And they came to Jesus and saw the demon-possessed  man, the one who had had the legion, sitting there, clothed and in his right mind, and they were afraid.
ESV.16: And those who had seen it described to them what had happened to the demon-possessed man and to the pigs.
ESV.17: And they began to beg Jesus  to depart from their region.
ESV.18: As he was getting into the boat, the man who had been possessed with demons begged him that he might be with him.
ESV.19: And he did not permit him but said to him, “Go home to your friends and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and how he has had mercy on you.”
ESV.20: And he went away and began to proclaim in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him, and everyone marveled.
ESV.21: And when Jesus had crossed again in the boat to the other side, a great crowd gathered about him, and he was beside the sea.
ESV.22: Then came one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name, and seeing him, he fell at his feet
ESV.23: and implored him earnestly, saying, “My little daughter is at the point of death. Come and lay your hands on her, so that she may be made well and live.”
ESV.24: And he went with him. And a great crowd followed him and thronged about him.
ESV.25: And there was a woman who had had a discharge of blood for twelve years,
ESV.26: and who had suffered much under many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was no better but rather grew worse.
ESV.27: She had heard the reports about Jesus and came up behind him in the crowd and touched his garment.
ESV.28: For she said, “If I touch even his garments, I will be made well.”
ESV.29: And immediately the flow of blood dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed of her disease.
ESV.30: And Jesus, perceiving in himself that power had gone out from him, immediately turned about in the crowd and said, “Who touched my garments?”
ESV.31: And his disciples said to him, “You see the crowd pressing around you, and yet you say, ‘Who touched me?’”
ESV.32: And he looked around to see who had done it.
ESV.33: But the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came in fear and trembling and fell down before him and told him the whole truth.
ESV.34: And he said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace, and be healed of your disease.”
ESV.35: While he was still speaking, there came from the ruler's house some who said, “Your daughter is dead. Why trouble the Teacher any further?”
ESV.36: But overhearing  what they said, Jesus said to the ruler of the synagogue, “Do not fear, only believe.”
ESV.37: And he allowed no one to follow him except Peter and James and John the brother of James.
ESV.38: They came to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and Jesus  saw a commotion, people weeping and wailing loudly.
ESV.39: And when he had entered, he said to them,  “Why are you making a commotion and weeping? The child is not dead but sleeping.”
ESV.40: And they laughed at him. But he put them all outside and took the child's father and mother and those who were with him and went in where the child was.
ESV.41: Taking her by the hand he said to her, “Talitha cumi,” which means, “Little girl, I say to you, arise.”
ESV.42: And immediately the girl got up and began walking (for she was twelve years of age), and they were immediately overcome with amazement.
ESV.43: And he strictly charged them that no one should know this, and told them to give her something to eat.

NASB.1: They came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gerasenes.
NASB.2: When He got out of the boat, immediately a man from the tombs with an unclean spirit met Him,
NASB.3: and he had his dwelling among the tombs. And no one was able to bind him anymore, even with a chain;
NASB.4: because he had often been bound with shackles and chains, and the chains had been torn apart by him and the shackles broken in pieces, and no one was strong enough to subdue him.
NASB.5: Constantly, night and day, he was screaming among the tombs and in the mountains, and gashing himself with stones.
NASB.6: Seeing Jesus from a distance, he ran up and bowed down before Him;
NASB.7: and shouting with a loud voice, he said, 'What business do we have with each other, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I implore You by God, do not torment me!'
NASB.8: For He had been saying to him, 'Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!'
NASB.9: And He was asking him, 'What is your name?' And he said to Him, 'My name is Legion; for we are many.'
NASB.10: And he [began] to implore Him earnestly not to send them out of the country.
NASB.11: Now there was a large herd of swine feeding nearby on the mountain.
NASB.12: [The demons] implored Him, saying, 'Send us into the swine so that we may enter them.'
NASB.13: Jesus gave them permission. And coming out, the unclean spirits entered the swine; and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea, about two thousand [of them]; and they were drowned in the sea.
NASB.14: Their herdsmen ran away and reported it in the city and in the country. And [the people] came to see what it was that had happened.
NASB.15: They came to Jesus and observed the man who had been demon-possessed sitting down, clothed and in his right mind, the very man who had had the 'legion'; and they became frightened.
NASB.16: Those who had seen it described to them how it had happened to the demon-possessed man, and [all] about the swine.
NASB.17: And they began to implore Him to leave their region.
NASB.18: As He was getting into the boat, the man who had been demon-possessed was imploring Him that he might accompany Him.
NASB.19: And He did not let him, but He said to him, 'Go home to your people and report to them what great things the Lord has done for you, and [how] He had mercy on you.'
NASB.20: And he went away and began to proclaim in Decapolis what great things Jesus had done for him; and everyone was amazed.
NASB.21: When Jesus had crossed over again in the boat to the other side, a large crowd gathered around Him; and so He stayed by the seashore.
NASB.22: One of the synagogue officials named Jairus came up, and on seeing Him, fell at His feet
NASB.23: and implored Him earnestly, saying, 'My little daughter is at the point of death; [please] come and lay Your hands on her, so that she will get well and live.'
NASB.24: And He went off with him; and a large crowd was following Him and pressing in on Him.
NASB.25: A woman who had had a hemorrhage for twelve years,
NASB.26: and had endured much at the hands of many physicians, and had spent all that she had and was not helped at all, but rather had grown worse--
NASB.27: after hearing about Jesus, she came up in the crowd behind [Him] and touched His cloak.
NASB.28: For she thought, 'If I just touch His garments, I will get well.'
NASB.29: Immediately the flow of her blood was dried up; and she felt in her body that she was healed of her affliction.
NASB.30: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in Himself that the power [proceeding] from Him had gone forth, turned around in the crowd and said, 'Who touched My garments?'
NASB.31: And His disciples said to Him, 'You see the crowd pressing in on You, and You say, 'Who touched Me?''
NASB.32: And He looked around to see the woman who had done this.
NASB.33: But the woman fearing and trembling, aware of what had happened to her, came and fell down before Him and told Him the whole truth.
NASB.34: And He said to her, 'Daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace and be healed of your affliction.'
NASB.35: While He was still speaking, they came from the [house of] the synagogue official, saying, 'Your daughter has died; why trouble the Teacher anymore?'
NASB.36: But Jesus, overhearing what was being spoken, said to the synagogue official, 'Do not be afraid [any] [longer], only believe.'
NASB.37: And He allowed no one to accompany Him, except Peter and James and John the brother of James.
NASB.38: They came to the house of the synagogue official; and He saw a commotion, and [people] loudly weeping and wailing.
NASB.39: And entering in, He said to them, 'Why make a commotion and weep? The child has not died, but is asleep.'
NASB.40: They [began] laughing at Him. But putting them all out, He took along the child's father and mother and His own companions, and entered [the room] where the child was.
NASB.41: Taking the child by the hand, He said to her, 'Talitha kum!' (which translated means, 'Little girl, I say to you, get up!').
NASB.42: Immediately the girl got up and [began] to walk, for she was twelve years old. And immediately they were completely astounded.
NASB.43: And He gave them strict orders that no one should know about this, and He said that [something] should be given her to eat.

NLT.1: So they arrived at the other side of the lake, in the region of the Gerasenes.
NLT.2: When Jesus climbed out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil spirit came out from the tombs to meet him.
NLT.3: This man lived in the burial caves and could no longer be restrained, even with a chain.
NLT.4: Whenever he was put into chains and shackles — as he often was — he snapped the chains from his wrists and smashed the shackles. No one was strong enough to subdue him.
NLT.5: Day and night he wandered among the burial caves and in the hills, howling and cutting himself with sharp stones.
NLT.6: When Jesus was still some distance away, the man saw him, ran to meet him, and bowed low before him.
NLT.7: With a shriek, he screamed, “Why are you interfering with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? In the name of God, I beg you, don’t torture me!”
NLT.8: For Jesus had already said to the spirit, “Come out of the man, you evil spirit.”
NLT.9: Then Jesus demanded, “What is your name?” And he replied, “My name is Legion, because there are many of us inside this man.”
NLT.10: Then the evil spirits begged him again and again not to send them to some distant place.
NLT.11: There happened to be a large herd of pigs feeding on the hillside nearby.
NLT.12: “Send us into those pigs,” the spirits begged. “Let us enter them.”
NLT.13: So Jesus gave them permission. The evil spirits came out of the man and entered the pigs, and the entire herd of about 2,000 pigs plunged down the steep hillside into the lake and drowned in the water.
NLT.14: The herdsmen fled to the nearby town and the surrounding countryside, spreading the news as they ran. People rushed out to see what had happened.
NLT.15: A crowd soon gathered around Jesus, and they saw the man who had been possessed by the legion of demons. He was sitting there fully clothed and perfectly sane, and they were all afraid.
NLT.16: Then those who had seen what happened told the others about the demon-possessed man and the pigs.
NLT.17: And the crowd began pleading with Jesus to go away and leave them alone.
NLT.18: As Jesus was getting into the boat, the man who had been demon possessed begged to go with him.
NLT.19: But Jesus said, “No, go home to your family, and tell them everything the Lord has done for you and how merciful he has been.”
NLT.20: So the man started off to visit the Ten Towns of that region and began to proclaim the great things Jesus had done for him; and everyone was amazed at what he told them.
NLT.21: Jesus got into the boat again and went back to the other side of the lake, where a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
NLT.22: Then a leader of the local synagogue, whose name was Jairus, arrived. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet,
NLT.23: pleading fervently with him. “My little daughter is dying,” he said. “Please come and lay your hands on her; heal her so she can live.”
NLT.24: Jesus went with him, and all the people followed, crowding around him.
NLT.25: A woman in the crowd had suffered for twelve years with constant bleeding.
NLT.26: She had suffered a great deal from many doctors, and over the years she had spent everything she had to pay them, but she had gotten no better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
NLT.27: She had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him through the crowd and touched his robe.
NLT.28: For she thought to herself, “If I can just touch his robe, I will be healed.”
NLT.29: Immediately the bleeding stopped, and she could feel in her body that she had been healed of her terrible condition.
NLT.30: Jesus realized at once that healing power had gone out from him, so he turned around in the crowd and asked, “Who touched my robe?”
NLT.31: His disciples said to him, “Look at this crowd pressing around you. How can you ask, ‘Who touched me?’”
NLT.32: But he kept on looking around to see who had done it.
NLT.33: Then the frightened woman, trembling at the realization of what had happened to her, came and fell to her knees in front of him and told him what she had done.
NLT.34: And he said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace. Your suffering is over.”
NLT.35: While he was still speaking to her, messengers arrived from the home of Jairus, the leader of the synagogue. They told him, “Your daughter is dead. There’s no use troubling the Teacher now.”
NLT.36: But Jesus overheard them and said to Jairus, “Don’t be afraid. Just have faith.”
NLT.37: Then Jesus stopped the crowd and wouldn’t let anyone go with him except Peter, James, and John (the brother of James).
NLT.38: When they came to the home of the synagogue leader, Jesus saw much commotion and weeping and wailing.
NLT.39: He went inside and asked, “Why all this commotion and weeping? The child isn’t dead; she’s only asleep.”
NLT.40: The crowd laughed at him. But he made them all leave, and he took the girl’s father and mother and his three disciples into the room where the girl was lying.
NLT.41: Holding her hand, he said to her, “Talitha koum,” which means “Little girl, get up!”
NLT.42: And the girl, who was twelve years old, immediately stood up and walked around! They were overwhelmed and totally amazed.
NLT.43: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell anyone what had happened, and then he told them to give her something to eat.

WEB.2: When he had come out of the boat, immediately a man with an unclean spirit met him out of the tombs.

WEB.15: They came to Jesus, and saw him who had been possessed by demons sitting, clothed, and in his right mind, even him who had the legion; and they were afraid.
WEB.16: Those who saw it declared to them what happened to him who was possessed by demons, and about the pigs.
WEB.17: They began to beg him to depart from their region.
WEB.18: As he was entering into the boat, he who had been possessed by demons begged him that he might be with him.
WEB.19: He didn’t allow him, but said to him, “Go to your house, to your friends, and tell them what great things the Lord has done for you, and how he had mercy on you.”
WEB.20: He went his way, and began to proclaim in Decapolis how Jesus had done great things for him, and everyone marveled.

WEB.25: A certain woman, who had an issue of blood for twelve years,

WEB.27: having heard the things concerning Jesus, came up behind him in the crowd, and touched his clothes.
WEB.28: For she said, “If I just touch his clothes, I will be made well.”

WEB.30: Immediately Jesus, perceiving in himself that the power had gone out from him, turned around in the crowd, and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”

WEB.33: But the woman, fearing and trembling, knowing what had been done to her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.
WEB.34: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace, and be cured of your disease.”
WEB.35: While he was still speaking, people came from the synagogue ruler’s house saying, “Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?”

WEB.37: He allowed no one to follow him, except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.
WEB.38: He came to the synagogue ruler’s house, and he saw an uproar, weeping, and great wailing.
WEB.39: When he had entered in, he said to them, “Why do you make an uproar and weep? The child is not dead, but is asleep.”
WEB.40: They ridiculed him. But he, having put them all out, took the father of the child, her mother, and those who were with him, and went in where the child was lying.
WEB.41: Taking the child by the hand, he said to her, “Talitha cumi!”  which means, being interpreted, “Girl, I tell you, get up!”

WEB.43: He strictly ordered them that no one should know this, and commanded that something should be given to her to eat.

AMP.1: They came to the other side of the sea, to the region of the Gerasenes.
AMP.2: When Jesus got out of the boat, immediately a man from the tombs with an unclean spirit met Him,
AMP.3: and the man lived in the tombs, and no one could bind him anymore, not even with chains.
AMP.4: For he had often been bound with shackles [for the feet] and with chains, and he tore apart the chains and broke the shackles into pieces, and no one was strong enough to subdue and tame him.
AMP.5: Night and day he was constantly screaming and shrieking among the tombs and on the mountains, and cutting himself with [sharp] stones.
AMP.6: Seeing Jesus from a distance, he ran up and bowed down before Him [in homage];
AMP.7: and screaming with a loud voice, he said, " What business do we have in common with each other, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I implore you by God [swear to me], do not torment me!"
AMP.8: For Jesus had been saying to him, "Come out of the man, you unclean spirit!"
AMP.9: He was asking him, "What is your name?" And he replied, "My name is Legion; for we are many."
AMP.10: And he began begging Him repeatedly not to send them out of the region.
AMP.11: Now there was a large herd of pigs grazing there on the mountain.
AMP.12: And the demons begged Him, saying, "Send us to the pigs so that we may go into them!"
AMP.13: Jesus gave them permission. And the unclean spirits came out [of the man] and entered the pigs. The herd, numbering about two thousand, rushed down the steep bank into the sea; and they were drowned [one after the other] in the sea.
AMP.14: The herdsmen [tending the pigs] ran away and reported it in the city and in the country. And the people came to see what had happened.
AMP.15: They came to Jesus and saw the man who had been demon-possessed sitting down, clothed and in his right mind, the man who had [previously] had the "legion" [of demons]; and they were frightened.
AMP.16: Those who had seen it described [in detail] to the people what had happened to the demon-possessed man, and [told them all] about the pigs.
AMP.17: So the people began to beg with Jesus to leave their region.
AMP.18: As He was stepping into the boat, the [Gentile] man who had been demon-possessed was begging with Him [asking] that he might go with Him [as a disciple].
AMP.19: Jesus did not let him [come], but [instead] He said to him, "Go home to your family and tell them all the great things that the Lord has done for you, and how He has had mercy on you."
AMP.20: So he [obeyed and] went away and began to publicly proclaim in Decapolis [the region of the ten Hellenistic cities] all the great things that Jesus had done for him; and all the people were astonished.
AMP.21: When Jesus had again crossed over in the boat to the other side [of the sea], a large crowd gathered around Him; and so He stayed by the seashore.
AMP.22: One of the synagogue officials named Jairus came up; and seeing Him, fell at His feet
AMP.23: and begged anxiously with Him, saying, "My little daughter is at the point of death; [please] come and lay Your hands on her, so that she will be healed and live."
AMP.24: And Jesus went with him; and a large crowd followed Him and pressed in around Him [from all sides].
AMP.25: A woman [in the crowd] had [suffered from] a hemorrhage for twelve years,
AMP.26: and had endured much [suffering] at the hands of many physicians. She had spent all that she had and was not helped at all, but instead had become worse.
AMP.27: She had heard [reports] about Jesus, and she came up behind Him in the crowd and touched His outer robe.
AMP.28: For she thought, "If I just touch His clothing, I will get well."
AMP.29: Immediately her flow of blood was dried up; and she felt in her body [and knew without any doubt] that she was healed of her suffering.
AMP.30: Immediately Jesus, recognizing in Himself that power had gone out from Him, turned around in the crowd and asked, "Who touched My clothes?"
AMP.31: His disciples said to Him, "You see the crowd pressing in around You [from all sides], and You ask, 'Who touched Me?'"
AMP.32: Still He kept looking around to see the woman who had done it.
AMP.33: And the woman, though she was afraid and trembling, aware of what had happened to her, came and fell down before Him and told Him the whole truth.
AMP.34: Then He said to her, "Daughter, your faith [your personal trust and confidence in Me] has restored you to health; go in peace and be [permanently] healed from your suffering."
AMP.35: While He was still speaking, some people came from the synagogue official's house, saying [to Jairus], "Your daughter has died; why bother the Teacher any longer?"
AMP.36: Overhearing what was being said, Jesus said to the synagogue official, "Do not be afraid; only keep on believing [in Me and my power]."
AMP.37: And He allowed no one to go with Him [as witnesses], except Peter and James and John the brother of James.
AMP.38: They came to the house of the synagogue official; and He looked [with understanding] at the uproar and commotion, and people loudly weeping and wailing [in mourning].
AMP.39: When He had gone in, He said to them, "Why make a commotion and weep? The child has not died, but is sleeping."
AMP.40: They began laughing [scornfully] at Him [because they knew the child was dead]. But He made them all go outside, and took along the child's father and mother and His own [three] companions, and entered the room where the child was.
AMP.41: Taking the child's hand, He said [tenderly] to her, "Talitha kum!"—which translated [from Aramaic] means, "Little girl, I say to you, get up!"
AMP.42: The little girl immediately got up and began to walk, for she was twelve years old. And immediately they [who witnessed the child's resurrection] were overcome with great wonder and utter amazement.
AMP.43: He gave strict orders that no one should know about this, and He told them to give her something to eat.

MSG.1: They arrived on the other side of the sea in the country of the Gerasenes.
MSG.2: As Jesus got out of the boat, a madman from the cemetery came up to him.
MSG.3: He lived there among the tombs and graves. No one could restrain him—he couldn't be chained, couldn't be tied down.
MSG.4: He had been tied up many times with chains and ropes, but he broke the chains, snapped the ropes. No one was strong enough to tame him.
MSG.5: Night and day he roamed through the graves and the hills, screaming out and slashing himself with sharp stones.
MSG.6: When he saw Jesus a long way off, he ran and bowed in worship before him—
MSG.7: then bellowed in protest, "What business do you have, Jesus, Son of the High God, messing with me? I swear to God, don't give me a hard time!"
MSG.8: (Jesus had just commanded the tormenting evil spirit, "Out! Get out of the man!")
MSG.9: Jesus asked him, "Tell me your name." He replied, "My name is Mob. I'm a rioting mob."
MSG.10: Then he desperately begged Jesus not to banish them from the country.
MSG.11: A large herd of pigs was browsing and rooting on a nearby hill.
MSG.12: The demons begged him, "Send us to the pigs so we can live in them."
MSG.13: Jesus gave the order. But it was even worse for the pigs than for the man. Crazed, they stampeded over a cliff into the sea and drowned.
MSG.14: Those tending the pigs, scared to death, bolted and told their story in town and country. Everyone wanted to see what had happened.
MSG.15: They came up to Jesus and saw the madman sitting there wearing decent clothes and making sense, no longer a walking madhouse of a man.
MSG.16: Those who had seen it told the others what had happened to the demon-possessed man and the pigs.
MSG.17: At first they were in awe—and then they were upset, upset over the drowned pigs. They demanded that Jesus leave and not come back.
MSG.18: As Jesus was getting into the boat, the demon-delivered man begged to go along,
MSG.19: but he wouldn't let him. Jesus said, "Go home to your own people. Tell them your story—what the Master did, how he had mercy on you."
MSG.20: The man went back and began to preach in the Ten Towns area about what Jesus had done for him. He was the talk of the town.
MSG.21: After Jesus crossed over by boat, a large crowd met him at the seaside.
MSG.22: One of the meeting-place leaders named Jairus came. When he saw Jesus, he fell to his knees,
MSG.23: beside himself as he begged, "My dear daughter is at death's door. Come and lay hands on her so she will get well and live."
MSG.24: Jesus went with him, the whole crowd tagging along, pushing and jostling him.
MSG.25: A woman who had suffered a condition of hemorrhaging for twelve years—
MSG.26: a long succession of physicians had treated her, and treated her badly, taking all her money and leaving her worse off than before—
MSG.27: had heard about Jesus. She slipped in from behind and touched his robe.
MSG.28: She was thinking to herself, "If I can put a finger on his robe, I can get well."
MSG.29: The moment she did it, the flow of blood dried up. She could feel the change and knew her plague was over and done with.
MSG.30: At the same moment, Jesus felt energy discharging from him. He turned around to the crowd and asked, "Who touched my robe?"
MSG.31: His disciples said, "What are you talking about? With this crowd pushing and jostling you, you're asking, 'Who touched me?' Dozens have touched you!"
MSG.32: But he went on asking, looking around to see who had done it.
MSG.33: The woman, knowing what had happened, knowing she was the one, stepped up in fear and trembling, knelt before him, and gave him the whole story.
MSG.34: Jesus said to her, "Daughter, you took a risk of faith, and now you're healed and whole. Live well, live blessed! Be healed of your plague."
MSG.35: While he was still talking, some people came from the leader's house and told him, "Your daughter is dead. Why bother the Teacher any more?"
MSG.36: Jesus overheard what they were talking about and said to the leader, "Don't listen to them; just trust me."
MSG.37: He permitted no one to go in with him except Peter, James, and John.
MSG.38: They entered the leader's house and pushed their way through the gossips looking for a story and neighbors bringing in casseroles.
MSG.39: Jesus was abrupt: "Why all this busybody grief and gossip? This child isn't dead; she's sleeping."
MSG.40: Provoked to sarcasm, they told him he didn't know what he was talking about. But when he had sent them all out, he took the child's father and mother, along with his companions, and entered the child's room.
MSG.41: He clasped the girl's hand and said, "Talitha koum," which means, "Little girl, get up."
MSG.42: At that, she was up and walking around! This girl was twelve years of age. They, of course, were all beside themselves with joy.
MSG.43: He gave them strict orders that no one was to know what had taken place in that room. Then he said, "Give her something to eat."

NIV.1:
The Healing of a Demon-possessed Man
They went across the lake to the region of the Gerasenes.
NIV.2: When Jesus got out of the boat, a man with an evil spirit came from the tombs to meet him.
NIV.3: This man lived in the tombs, and no one could bind him any more, not even with a chain.
NIV.4: For he had often been chained hand and foot, but he tore the chains apart and broke the irons on his feet. No one was strong enough to subdue him.
NIV.5: Night and day among the tombs and in the hills he would cry out and cut himself with stones.
NIV.6: When he saw Jesus from a distance, he ran and fell on his knees in front of him.
NIV.7: He shouted at the top of his voice, “What do you want with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? Swear to God that you won’t torture me!”
NIV.8: For Jesus had said to him, “Come out of this man, you evil spirit!”
NIV.9:
Then Jesus asked him, “What is your name?”
“My name is Legion,” he replied, “for we are many.”
NIV.10: And he begged Jesus again and again not to send them out of the area.
NIV.11: A large herd of pigs was feeding on the nearby hillside.
NIV.12: The demons begged Jesus, “Send us among the pigs; allow us to go into them.”
NIV.13: He gave them permission, and the evil spirits came out and went into the pigs. The herd, about two thousand in number, rushed down the steep bank into the lake and were drowned.
NIV.14: Those tending the pigs ran off and reported this in the town and countryside, and the people went out to see what had happened.
NIV.15: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had been possessed by the legion of demons, sitting there, dressed and in his right mind; and they were afraid.
NIV.16: Those who had seen it told the people what had happened to the demon-possessed man—and told about the pigs as well.
NIV.17: Then the people began to plead with Jesus to leave their region.
NIV.18: As Jesus was getting into the boat, the man who had been demon-possessed begged to go with him.
NIV.19: Jesus did not let him, but said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you, and how he has had mercy on you.”
NIV.20: So the man went away and began to tell in the Decapolis how much Jesus had done for him. And all the people were amazed.
NIV.21:
A Dead Girl and a Sick Woman
When Jesus had again crossed over by boat to the other side of the lake, a large crowd gathered around him while he was by the lake.
NIV.22: Then one of the synagogue rulers, named Jairus, came there. Seeing Jesus, he fell at his feet
NIV.23: and pleaded earnestly with him, “My little daughter is dying. Please come and put your hands on her so that she will be healed and live.”
NIV.24:
So Jesus went with him.
A large crowd followed and pressed around him.
NIV.25: And a woman was there who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years.
NIV.26: She had suffered a great deal under the care of many doctors and had spent all she had, yet instead of getting better she grew worse.
NIV.27: When she heard about Jesus, she came up behind him in the crowd and touched his cloak,
NIV.28: because she thought, “If I just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
NIV.29: Immediately her bleeding stopped and she felt in her body that she was freed from her suffering.
NIV.30: At once Jesus realized that power had gone out from him. He turned around in the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
NIV.31: “You see the people crowding against you,” his disciples answered, “and yet you can ask, ‘Who touched me?’ ”
NIV.32: But Jesus kept looking around to see who had done it.
NIV.33: Then the woman, knowing what had happened to her, came and fell at his feet and, trembling with fear, told him the whole truth.
NIV.34: He said to her, “Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace and be freed from your suffering.”
NIV.35: While Jesus was still speaking, some men came from the house of Jairus, the synagogue ruler. “Your daughter is dead,” they said. “Why bother the teacher any more?”
NIV.36: Ignoring what they said, Jesus told the synagogue ruler, “Don’t be afraid; just believe.”
NIV.37: He did not let anyone follow him except Peter, James and John the brother of James.
NIV.38: When they came to the home of the synagogue ruler, Jesus saw a commotion, with people crying and wailing loudly.
NIV.39: He went in and said to them, “Why all this commotion and wailing? The child is not dead but asleep.”
NIV.40:
But they laughed at him.
After he put them all out, he took the child’s father and mother and the disciples who were with him, and went in where the child was.
NIV.41: He took her by the hand and said to her, “Talitha koum!” (which means, “Little girl, I say to you, get up!”).
NIV.42: Immediately the girl stood up and walked around (she was twelve years old). At this they were completely astonished.
NIV.43: He gave strict orders not to let anyone know about this, and told them to give her something to eat.
